arts.ttl: Fabrizio Romano Provides Key Update on PSG Target Amid Barcelona, Real Madrid Interest
arts.sum: Paris Saint-Germain’s 2024-25 season will be pivotal in determining whether they truly have a No. 9 issue. This summer, the club chose to put its faith in Gonçalo Ramos and Randal Kolo Muani and decided not to pursue another striker. arts.sum: Randal Kolo Muani (25) has found game time hard to come by at Paris Saint-Germain this season. Whilst he has made nine appearances in all competitions, only two of those…

arts.ttl: PLAYER RATINGS | OGC Nice 1-1 PSG: Luis Enrique’s men held as Monaco remain top of Ligue 1
arts.sum: Ligue 1 McDonald’s, Round 7, 06/10/24 Paris Saint-Germain were held to a draw (1-1) by OGC Nice on Sunday night, allowing AS Monaco to remain top of Ligue 1 after…

arts.ttl: Aston Villa’s €35M-Rated Standout Attracted Attention From Bayern Munich and PSG, Report Says
arts.sum: Paris Saint-Germain was rumored to be pursuing a striker all summer transfer window. From the likes of Victor Osimhen and Benjamin Šeško, the gossip linked the French giants to a few No. 9’s. Despite opting to go with Gonçalo Ramos and Randal Kolo Muani, the Ligue 1 side had another striker in mind to strengthen […] The post Aston Villa’s €35M-Rated Standout Attracted Attention From Bayern Munich and PSG, Report Says appeared first on PSG Talk.

arts.ttl: Video: Ousmane Dembélé Puts Ball on Tee for Nuno Mendes’ Stunning Goal
arts.sum: After conceding a goal in the first half, Paris Saint-Germain have responded. Ousmane Dembélé put the ball on a tee for Nuno Mendes who fired off a shot from outside the box to beat OGC Nice goalkeeper Marcin Bułka to level the scoring. The Parisians had an underwhelming first half, but coming out of halftime, […] The post Video: Ousmane Dembélé Puts Ball on Tee for Nuno Mendes’ Stunning Goal appeared first on PSG Talk.

arts.ttl: PSG identify possible Gianluigi Donnarumma replacement following concerning performances
arts.sum: PSG are concerned with their goalkeeping options and have therefore identified Manchester City’s Ederson as a possible transfer target. Luis Enrique has three senior goalkeepers among his options including Italy international Gianluigi Donnarumma. The 6’4″ shot-stopper has been at the Parc des Princes since his controversial free transfer from AC Milan in 2021. However, although […] The post PSG identify possible Gianluigi Donnarumma replacement following concerning performances appeared first on CaughtOffside.

arts.ttl: Ousmane Dembélé back in PSG squad to take on Nice in French league
arts.sum: PSG coach Luis Enrique was criticized after the loss for not selecting Dembélé, one of the most creative players in the team following the departure of Kylian Mbappé to Real Madrid. “There is just a problem of commitment toward the team, it’s not a problem between the player and the coach,” Enrique said through a translator.

arts.ttl: Samuele Ricci: “Manchester City and the national team? It’s nice, but I’m just thinking about Improving”
arts.sum: Torino midfielder Samuele Ricci addressed recent speculation about his future following his team’s 3-2 away loss to Inter Milan. Despite growing interest from Manchester City and potential national team call-ups, Ricci remains grounded and focused on his development. Reflecting on the match against Inter, Ricci said, “It was a bit like the game we played in Verona: that time there was a red card in our favor, tonight against us. These things can happen, football is also this. After the sending off, we continued to stay in the game, and we take home some positives.” When asked about handling the The post Samuele Ricci: “Manchester City and the national team? It’s nice, but I’m just thinking about Improving” appeared first on FootItalia.com.

arts.ttl: PSG’s Defeat to Arsenal Shows ‘Men Against Boys’ Maturity Difference, Expert Says
arts.sum: Paris Saint-Germain lost 2-0 to Arsenal on Tuesday night at Emirates Stadium, and just days later, one expert described the match as one in which men played against boys. PSG struggled after conceding two first-half goals, with Gianluigi Donnarumma facing criticism for key mistakes.
